cards.title: Anthropic's Claude Tag Joins Slack as Always-On AI
cards.body: Anthropic launches Claude Tag, an AI teammate integrated directly into Slack for real-time collaboration. The feature captures organizational context and institutional knowledge, positioning itself as a strategic enterprise play.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Open Source Bug-Patching Initiative
cards.body: OpenAI is deploying AI to help the open source community identify and fix security vulnerabilities. The initiative strengthens collective defenses across the ecosystem.

cards.title: Fika Jobs Raises $4M for Video-First Hiring
cards.body: Stockholm-based Fika Jobs secured $4M to build an AI-powered hiring platform combining video interviews with short-form profiles. The platform merges LinkedIn and TikTok dynamics for candidate assessment.

cards.title: Tech Layoffs Linked to AI Continue in 2026
cards.body: Major tech companies continue announcing significant workforce reductions citing AI as a stated factor. TechCrunch tracks the ongoing trend in reverse chronological order.

cards.title: AI World Gets 'Loopy' With Autonomous Agents
cards.body: The emerging 'loop' trend pushes agentic AI further by authorizing swarms of agents to work continuously in the background. This represents the next evolution in autonomous system design.

cards.title: Groq Confirms $650M Raise After Nvidia Deal
cards.body: AI chipmaker Groq secured $650M funding and is restaffing following Nvidia's $20B acquisition. The company is doubling down on its neocloud business and hiring new executives.

cards.title: Nvidia's Water Solution Doesn't Fix AI Problem
cards.body: Nvidia announced a cooling system reducing data center water consumption, but critics say it ignores AI's largest water drain—fossil fuel power plants. The announcement addresses internal efficiency, not industry-wide sustainability.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Invests $75M in AI Filmmaking
cards.body: Google DeepMind and A24 partner to develop AI-powered filmmaking tools, marking a major bet on AI's Hollywood future. The collaboration aims to create new creative possibilities for production.

cards.title: Amazon Tests Alexa+ Hindi Version in India
cards.body: Amazon is expanding its conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India with Hindi-language support. The company is inviting Indian users to test the localized version.

cards.title: SpaceX, Reflection AI Ink $150M Monthly Compute Deal
cards.body: Open source AI lab Reflection AI will pay $150M monthly for access to Nvidia's latest GB300 chips through SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis. The deal runs from July 2026 through 2029.

cards.title: Trump Administration Cracks Down on Anthropic
cards.body: The administration has taken action against Anthropic, prompting discussion about broader implications for the AI ecosystem. Industry observers debate what prompted the latest moves.

cards.title: iOS 27 Brings Practical AI Features Beyond Siri
cards.body: While Siri's AI overhaul dominated WWDC headlines, Apple is rolling out equally useful AI features throughout iOS 27. These tools focus on practical user productivity gains.

cards.title: Signal's Meredith Whittaker: Chatbots Aren't Friends
cards.body: Signal's Meredith Whittaker warns against anthropomorphizing AI, stressing that chatbots are not conscious beings or sentient companions. The reminder pushes back against misleading framing of AI capabilities.

cards.title: DeepMind's John Jumper Joins Anthropic
cards.body: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving Google DeepMind for rival Anthropic, part of a larger exodus of talent. The move signals shifting dynamics in the competitive AI research landscape.

cards.title: US Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Models
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to withdraw Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns following guardrail bypass discoveries by Amazon researchers. The ban marks a significant regulatory intervention in AI model releases.

cards.title: Anthropic Ban Paradoxically Boosts Brand Momentum
cards.body: Despite the US government's forced model withdrawal, market metrics suggest Anthropic's brand is gaining traction. The controversy may be inadvertently strengthening the company's profile.

cards.title: Export Controls Haven't Stopped Cybersecurity Software
cards.body: Three decades of export restrictions on cybersecurity-related software have proven largely ineffective historically. Questions remain whether similar controls will succeed with Anthropic's Mythos model.

cards.title: Anthropic's Claude Tag Brings AI to Slack
cards.body: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, an always-on AI teammate for Slack that learns organizational context and institutional knowledge. The feature represents a strategic move to capture enterprise workflows and integrate AI deeper into workplace tools.

cards.title: Fika Jobs Raises $4M for AI Interview Platform
cards.body: Stockholm-based Fika Jobs built a video-first hiring platform combining AI interview agents with short-form video profiles. The startup blends LinkedIn's professional networking with TikTok's video-first approach for candidate screening.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Open Source Bug-Patching Initiative
cards.body: OpenAI is leveraging AI to help the open source community identify and patch vulnerabilities. The initiative strengthens security infrastructure across widely-used projects.

cards.title: Agentic AI Gets 'Loopy' With Continuous Swarms
cards.body: The loop concept pushes agentic AI further by enabling swarms of agents to work continuously in the background. This endless autonomous operation represents the next evolution in AI agent capabilities.

cards.title: Groq Confirms $650M Raise, Expands Leadership
cards.body: AI chipmaker Groq secured $650 million in funding following Nvidia's $20 billion talent acquisition deal. The company is strengthening its neocloud business and hiring new executives to compete in the AI chip space.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Invests $75M in AI Filmmaking
cards.body: Google DeepMind partnered with A24 to develop AI tools for filmmaking. The $75 million bet signals the company's push into creative AI applications for Hollywood.

cards.title: Amazon Tests Alexa+ Conversational AI in India
cards.body: Amazon is expanding its new Alexa+ conversational AI assistant to India with Hindi language support. The expansion tests the platform's multilingual capabilities in a major market.

cards.title: SpaceX Partners With Reflection AI on Compute
cards.body: Reflection AI secured a compute deal with SpaceX worth $150 million monthly for access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips. The arrangement runs through 2029 at SpaceX's Memphis data center.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: Nobel Prize-winning researcher John Jumper departed Google DeepMind to join rival Anthropic. His move reflects ongoing talent migration and competition in the AI research sector.

cards.title: Apple Rolls Out Practical AI in iOS 27
cards.body: Apple revealed multiple AI features coming to iOS 27 beyond Siri's overhaul announced at WWDC. The updates integrate AI capabilities across iPhone apps and system functions.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker: AI Chatbots Aren't Friends
cards.body: Signal president Meredith Whittaker emphasized that AI chatbots are tools, not conscious beings or friends. Her commentary pushes back against anthropomorphizing AI systems.

cards.title: Export Controls Unlikely to Stop Anthropic Models
cards.body: Recent export controls attempt to restrict Anthropic's Mythos cybersecurity model, echoing 30 years of ineffective software restrictions. History suggests such measures rarely succeed in preventing technology distribution.

cards.title: Anthropic's Government Ban Strengthens Brand
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security concerns. The controversy may paradoxically enhance Anthropic's market perception and brand awareness.

cards.title: Subquadratic Claims LLM Bottleneck Breakthrough
cards.body: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic claims it solved a mathematical bottleneck limiting large language models for nearly a decade. The stealth-mode announcement lacks full technical details but suggests major LLM advances.

cards.title: Brain-Computer Interface Trials Accelerate
cards.body: Brain-computer interface trials are expanding, with patients like Casey Harrell using implants for years to restore communication and control. These devices are proving transformative for people with paralysis and ALS.

cards.title: How Anthropic Triggered Its Own Export Ban
cards.body: Anthropic's public statements and research disclosures may have inadvertently prompted the US government's export ban on its latest models. The company's transparency strategy backfired with regulatory consequences.
